evidence.display: 57-year-old gentleman with CLL and aggressive large B-cell lymphoma s/p    allo
evidence.text: 57-year-old gentleman with CLL and aggressive large B-cell lymphoma s/p    allo SCT in [**2-24**] w/ AMS and hypothermia in the setting of BK viremia.

evidence.display: Transferred to MICU on [**9-10**] after resuscitation for PEA arrest the    floo
evidence.text: Transferred to MICU on [**9-10**] after resuscitation for PEA arrest the    floor.

evidence.display: Pt was intubated and required pressor support following arrest.
evidence.text: Pt was intubated and required pressor support following arrest.

evidence.display: Pressors off since [**9-12**] and pt was extubated on [**9-14**].
evidence.text: Pressors off since [**9-12**] and pt was extubated on [**9-14**].

evidence.display: RE-INTUBATED ON    [**2124-9-16**] FOLLOWED BY CARDIAC AREEST.
evidence.text: RE-INTUBATED ON    [**2124-9-16**] FOLLOWED BY CARDIAC AREEST.

evidence.display: Events;cardiac arrest,intubated.EKG,Xray, rt groin CVL placement    Bronchoscopy
evidence.text: Events;cardiac arrest,intubated.EKG,Xray, rt groin CVL placement    Bronchoscopy.,multiple ABG.     cardiac arrest    Assessment:    Pt does not respond for verbal commands @ baseline ,moans and  agitated    at times with periods of quite time in between,noted Systolic pressure    to 160-190 with agitation,HR stable,sats 100 % on RA.    @ 0051 noted that pt was unresponsive bardicardic HR  to 20- 30 s NBP    not recordable.
Witnessed PEA arrest.

evidence.display: Action:    Team informed, activated code blue,CPR,epi X 1 with return of    puls
evidence.text: Action:    Team informed, activated code blue,CPR,epi X 1 with return of    pulse,intubated by anaesthesia vented on AC 100/500/20/5,pacer pads are    on and atropine and epi @ bedside.
Central line placement,NS 1L wide open,EKG.

evidence.display: Multiple ABG and  lab work,CXray.lytes replacement.
evidence.text: Multiple ABG and  lab work,CXray.lytes replacement.

evidence.display: Wife was [**Name (NI) 167**] by the team, @ bedside  now.
evidence.text: Wife was [**Name (NI) 167**] by the team, @ bedside  now.

evidence.display: Response:    Regained the pulse, suctioned mucus plugs after intubation    Tube
evidence.text: Response:    Regained the pulse, suctioned mucus plugs after intubation    Tube placement confirmed with Xray.

evidence.display: Continued to be bradicardic 30-50 atropine 0.5 mg/IV with good effect.(    rate
evidence.text: Continued to be bradicardic 30-50 atropine 0.5 mg/IV with good effect.(    rate was not coordinating with rhythm,so leads changed )HR remains    stable for the last 4 hrs.

evidence.display: Plan:    Summaraised the event to pts wife  by the MD and this RN    Would like
evidence.text: Plan:    Summaraised the event to pts wife  by the MD and this RN    Would like to continue as Full Code.

evidence.display: Respiratory failure, acute (not ARDS/[**Doctor Last Name 11**])    Assessment:
evidence.text: Respiratory failure, acute (not ARDS/[**Doctor Last Name 11**])    Assessment:    Pt intubated in the context of PEA arrest,cxr  with haziness @ left    lung or no  flash PE.

evidence.display: Lungs essentially clear on auscultation but    diminished at the bases.
evidence.text: Lungs essentially clear on auscultation but    diminished at the bases.

evidence.display: Remains vented on AC 100/500/20/5    Action:    No vent changes made.
evidence.text: Remains vented on AC 100/500/20/5    Action:    No vent changes made.
Resp status monitored closely.

evidence.display: MS assessed    frequently    Sedated with propofol 20 mic/kg/mt    Bronchoscopy
evidence.text: MS assessed    frequently    Sedated with propofol 20 mic/kg/mt    Bronchoscopy at bedside showed marked airway collapse didn t reveal any    mucus plugs.

evidence.display: Response:    Comfortable on current vent settings    Multiple ABG please refer f
evidence.text: Response:    Comfortable on current vent settings    Multiple ABG please refer flow sheet for details.

evidence.display: Plan:    Continue to monitor pt s resp and MS    Wean sedations and vent as tole
evidence.text: Plan:    Continue to monitor pt s resp and MS    Wean sedations and vent as tolerated.

evidence.display: Altered mental status (not Delirium)    Assessment:    Patient was non verbal, c
evidence.text: Altered mental status (not Delirium)    Assessment:    Patient was non verbal, continued to moan and grimace,  and was able to    follow simple commands  at times.

evidence.display: Pupils  3 mm equal and reactive,    strong upper extr.
evidence.text: Pupils  3 mm equal and reactive,    strong upper extr.

evidence.display: Action:    Mental status checks q4hr and prn. Now sedated with propofol 20 mic a
evidence.text: Action:    Mental status checks q4hr and prn. Now sedated with propofol 20 mic after intubation.

evidence.display: Response:    No changes in mental status    Plan:    Continue to monitor mental
evidence.text: Response:    No changes in mental status    Plan:    Continue to monitor mental status and pain.

evidence.display: Impaired Skin Integrity    Assessment:    Patient w/anasarca, multiple areas of
evidence.text: Impaired Skin Integrity    Assessment:    Patient w/anasarca, multiple areas of blisters (broken and whole), B/L    LE w/abrasions (scratches like), weeping UE and LE.

evidence.text: Deep tissue injury    on the buttocks and between the gluteal folds.

evidence.text: Response:    Ongoing    Plan:    Continue to reassess skin integrity, turn/reposition q2hr and prn. f/u    wound care recs.
